steps.m: to be distracted or diverted from something important
steps.n: This form makes the action into a noun

steps.n: Added كُمْ to show the recipients are multiple people being addressed. This verb connects to أُجُورَكُمْ (your rewards) which comes right after it in the ayah.

steps.n: Added كُمْ (kum) at the end to mean 'you all' as the receivers of the action

steps.n: The notes don't match - this word already has the plural 'you' suffix كم built in. The main difference is in meaning (press/urge vs welcome), not structure.

steps.m: to spend or give away (especially money or resources)

Changed from 'he spent' to 'you spend' by adding تُ at start and changing middle vowel to ِ (kasra)
Added وا to show multiple people are doing the action

steps.m: so that you all may spend
